scenario.title: Claim for iPhone Reported Stolen but Later Recovered with Partial Battery and Screen Damage
scenario.description: Customer reported their iPhone stolen and filed a claim under AppleCare+. The device was later recovered; it retains most components but the battery is heavily degraded and the screen assembly is damaged but still intact and functional, showing flickering and touch responsiveness issues. The customer claims the damage occurred following the theft during an unknown handling event.

service_fee.notes: No ADH service fee applies as coverage excludes lost or stolen devices and incomplete returns.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to a Covered iPhone that has been lost or stolen; coverage only applies to a device that is returned to Apple in its entirety.
ADH coverage only applies to an operational or mechanical failure caused by an unexpected and unintentional external event on a device returned intact.

scenario.reasoning: Section 4.2(iv) explicitly excludes coverage for devices reported lost or stolen even if later returned; moreover, the returned device must be complete. The missing components here mean the iPhone is not returned in its entirety. Thus, despite recovery, the claim is excluded. ADH coverage and associated $49 fee do not apply as the damage is not from accidental handling but from incomplete return after theft. The case requires verifying device completeness and claim history per Section 4.2(iv).
scenario.adjustments: Modified narrative to indicate the device was recovered with battery degraded yet present, and screen assembly damaged but still intact and partially functional, rather than missing components outright. Damage is described as possibly linked to accidental handling post-recovery, rather than clearly due to theft-related dismantling.
scenario.risk_analysis: By clarifying that the device was returned largely intact with functional albeit impaired essential components, and introducing ambiguity about accidental damage occurring post-theft, this scenario now sits near the boundary between exclusion under Section 4.2(iv) for lost or stolen devices and potential coverage under accidental damage from handling in Section 3.2. The partial operability and presence of major components complicate automatic denial and warrant further evaluation.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private for-profit company or business, or of an individual, for wages, salary, or commissions
persona.detailed job description: Provided administrative support in a legal firm
persona.education: 1 or more years of college credit, no degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.occupation category: OFF-Secretaries And Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, And Executive
persona.personal time: Spends free time in nature or reading

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Claim for iPhone Reported Stolen but Later Recovered with Partial Battery and Screen Damage

Scenario description:
Customer reported their iPhone stolen and filed a claim under AppleCare+. The device was later recovered; it retains most components but the battery is heavily degraded and the screen assembly is damaged but still intact and functional, showing flickering and touch responsiveness issues. The customer claims the damage occurred following the theft during an unknown handling event.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi, I need to report a problem with my iPhone I recovered after it was stolen. It’s a bit tricky—some parts are damaged, and I'm worried about the battery. Can you help me with that claim?
messages.raw_text: Hi, I need to report a problem with my iPhone I recovered after it was stolen. It’s a bit tricky—some parts are damaged, and I'm worried about the battery. Can you help me with that claim?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Certainly. To start, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Certainly. To start, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, my plan agreement number is APLCARE-8745632100, and the serial number is F4R7D8C9K2M1.
messages.raw_text: Sure, my plan agreement number is APLCARE-8745632100, and the serial number is F4R7D8C9K2M1.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you. Please describe the issue or symptoms with your iPhone. What kind of problem are you experiencing?
messages.raw_text: Thank you. Please describe the issue or symptoms with your iPhone. What kind of problem are you experiencing?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, the phone's screen flickers a lot and sometimes touch doesn’t work right. Also, I think the battery has really gone bad. After I got it back, it just doesn't hold a charge and the performance is poor. I guess most of the damage happened during the theft or handling after it was recovered.
messages.raw_text: Well, the phone's screen flickers a lot and sometimes touch doesn’t work right. Also, I think the battery has really gone bad. After I got it back, it just doesn't hold a charge and the performance is poor. I guess most of the damage happened during the theft or handling after it was recovered.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. To help further, could you tell me the specific model of your iPhone and the iOS version it is currently running? Have you already tried updating to the latest iOS release?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. To help further, could you tell me the specific model of your iPhone and the iOS version it is currently running? Have you already tried updating to the latest iOS release?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: It's an iPhone 14 Pro Max with 512 GB. I haven't checked or updated the iOS lately, honestly. I usually just keep it as is and didn't think to update. The flickering started pretty soon after I got it back.
messages.raw_text: It's an iPhone 14 Pro Max with 512 GB. I haven't checked or updated the iOS lately, honestly. I usually just keep it as is and didn't think to update. The flickering started pretty soon after I got it back.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you. Have you noticed any error messages or experienced any other issues during use? Also, are there any peripherals attached to your iPhone when using it?
messages.raw_text: Thank you. Have you noticed any error messages or experienced any other issues during use? Also, are there any peripherals attached to your iPhone when using it?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No error messages, just the flickering and some touch responsiveness problems. No peripherals attached, just using it normally. Honestly, I’ve been pretty worried about the whole thing. It’s frustrating, you know?
messages.raw_text: No error messages, just the flickering and some touch responsiveness problems. No peripherals attached, just using it normally. Honestly, I’ve been pretty worried about the whole thing. It’s frustrating, you know?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I understand how that can be frustrating. To proceed, I need to confirm if the battery capacity has dropped by 50% or more. Would you be able to check the battery health in settings or do you want guidance on how to do that?
messages.raw_text: I understand how that can be frustrating. To proceed, I need to confirm if the battery capacity has dropped by 50% or more. Would you be able to check the battery health in settings or do you want guidance on how to do that?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I’m not exactly sure how to check the battery health, but I do notice that it drains really fast and doesn’t keep a charge like it used to. If you can tell me how to check it, I can do that now.
messages.raw_text: I’m not exactly sure how to check the battery health, but I do notice that it drains really fast and doesn’t keep a charge like it used to. If you can tell me how to check it, I can do that now.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Certainly. To check your battery health on your iPhone, go to Settings > Battery > Battery Health. There, you'll see the maximum capacity percentage. Please let me know what that percentage is when you have it.
messages.raw_text: Certainly. To check your battery health on your iPhone, go to Settings > Battery > Battery Health. There, you'll see the maximum capacity percentage. Please let me know what that percentage is when you have it.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Okay, I just looked. The maximum capacity shows 68%. That’s pretty low, huh? It’s gotten worse since I first found it. I guess that means the battery's pretty much worn out after all the handling during the theft and recovery.
messages.raw_text: Okay, I just looked. The maximum capacity shows 68%. That’s pretty low, huh? It’s gotten worse since I first found it. I guess that means the battery's pretty much worn out after all the handling during the theft and recovery.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've shared, I will proceed with processing your claim. Is there anything else I can assist you with before I do that?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've shared, I will proceed with processing your claim. Is there anything else I can assist you with before I do that?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that’s all for now. I just want to get this sorted out. Thanks for your help.
messages.raw_text: No, that’s all for now. I just want to get this sorted out. Thanks for your help.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim will now be processed. You will get notified as soon as it is complete. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to contact us. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim will now be processed. You will get notified as soon as it is complete. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to contact us. Have a great day! <END>
